mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 when UNRATE crosses the 5.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by >0.5% over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects tightening capacity utilization and rising corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant with p > 0.10 during mid-cycle expansion, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher aggregate income, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months with positive PAYEMS growth, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4, or the threshold signal shows no predictive alpha with Rank IC below 0.03 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising unemployment signals a deepening economic contraction, which suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to achieve statistical significance with p > 0.05 in multivariate regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth falling below 2.0 percent year-over-year indicates supply-side stagnation that constrains corporate output and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during slowdown regimes.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive forward returns in >60% of triggered months, or predictive accuracy with Rank IC drops below 0.03 when applied to periods classified as growth slowdowns.
